commit_scope: Add an OSI-shaped native channel-flow protocol surface in BML, preserve consent-interface flow receipts, and align the public Form/channel architecture docs to it.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/channel-interface.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/channel-flow.fk form-stdlib/tests/channel-flow-band.fk
cd form && ./validate.sh --binary form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/channel-interface.fk form-stdlib/kernel-http.fk form-stdlib/channel-flow.fk form-stdlib/tests/channel-flow-band.fk

results.wellness: pass; production aligned with origin/main; kernel-native API reading remains 22/812 kernel-served and 23 kernel-first capable
results.channel_flow_source: 8388607; Go/Rust/TypeScript agree on complete seven-layer HTTP flow, phase counts, Allow header, HEAD bridge pressure, route candidate pressure, consent-interface receipts, and bilateral invasion counts
results.channel_flow_binary: 8388607; Go/Rust/TypeScript agree on every binary artifact
results.blueprint_scan: pass; no unregistered type-99 NodeIDs or unregistered bp names; pre-existing name-drift warnings remain visible
results.readme_generation: pass; README.md, CLI README, MCP README, and coherence-network skill regenerated from source templates

ci_validation.notes: Remote CI is pending until this branch is pushed and a PR is opened.

deploy_validation.notes: No production deployment changed in this commit; behavior is covered by Form source and binary sibling-kernel validation.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The native channel surface now has a BML-authored OSI flow cell model. HTTP is represented as one application-layer channel-flow profile whose policy remains the real kernel-http kh-channel-policy, so future carriers can add profiles without forking the host router.
test_flows: Source validation proves cf-http-channel-flow has seven ordered OSI layers, gas/water/ice phase observability, delegates HTTP policy to kh-channel-policy, and keeps interface-flow honor/invasion receipts executable.
Binary validation proves the same content-addressed artifact behavior across Go/Rust/TypeScript.
Blueprint scan proves CHANNEL-OSI-LAYER and CHANNEL-FLOW are registered and visible through all generated bp tables.
gaps_found_and_closed: The protocol docs still described channel.fk and sockets as future; updated them to current shipped shape.
The native HTTP policy previously stood alone; channel-flow now gives it an OSI L7 home while preserving policy reuse and the receiver-offer consent gate.
The human blueprint registry had channel breath cells but not the new OSI flow cells; added 1702 and 1703 with proof.
